summary: Gruber makes his case for direct reader support of Daring Fireball as the membership and T-shirt drive reaches its deadline. He explains why advertising-based revenue would compromise the site's quality, arguing that ads incentivize quantity over depth. He acknowledges the Catch-22 of needing revenue to build features while readers want features before paying. The post is both a fundraising pitch and a manifesto for independent, reader-supported journalism that prioritizes nuance and detail over mass appeal.
key_points: Advertising revenue models incentivize quantity (more page views) while readers value quality (depth, nuance, attention to detail)
A one-man publication can't maintain the editorial-advertising wall that larger media companies use to preserve independence
There's a Catch-22 in building a membership site: you need revenue to build features, but readers want features before they'll pay
Direct reader support had already outperformed AdSense revenue but still fell short of a full-time income
Gruber positioned Daring Fireball as serving readers unsatisfied with typical tech media journalism
money_quotes: What's most appealing to advertisers is quantity. More readers, more hits. More traffic. More page views.
What most appealing to you, the regular readers of this site, is quality. Attention to detail. Appreciation of nuance. Depth.
An advertising revenue model leads to trying to appeal a little bit to very many people.
What I'm trying to do is appeal very much to fewer people — in particular, people who aren't satisfied with typical tech media journalism.
People who appreciate that some ideas are subtle, and take more than a few paragraphs to express.
Most publications maintain at least some semblence of a wall between the advertising and editorial departments; such a wall obviously isn't plausible for a one-man show.

I can't promise to add features without the revenue, but you — the reader sitting on the fence with regard to coughing up the money to join — are unlikely to be motivated by these as-yet-unimplemented features until they're actually available.
key_insight: Advertising-supported media optimizes for breadth while reader-supported media can optimize for depth, making direct funding the only viable model for independent writing that values nuance over traffic.
